Building ecosystems, writing essays, hosting stages. Programs designer, researcher, radio host, event anchor, and essayist from Akwa Ibom, Nigeria.

Scroll

About

Polymath from the Niger Delta

Ikanke-Abasi Umana is a programs and operations professional, researcher, writer, and event host based in Akwa Ibom State, Nigeria. She holds a B.A. in Communication Arts from the University of Uyo, graduating as Best Student of the 2019/2020 session with a 4.14 GPA — the highest in her class.

She currently serves as Product Integration & Growth Manager and TalentWorks Venture Lead at Mosaic Human Capital Innovations (MHCI) — building operational systems, leading product-market strategy, running the TalentWorks talent placement venture, designing training curricula, and managing multi-stakeholder partnerships. She is also Co-Founder of Founder Advocacy Network (FAN).

She has been hosting events since she was a child — children's day programmes and teens conventions at her parents' church, Model UN director in secondary school, to tech festival stages and founder meetups. Her range is wide by design. When something needs doing — she steps forward.

4+Years in programs and operations
2Research papers (one in publication process)
30+Events hosted or produced end-to-end
3yrOn Inspiration FM as radio host

The Work

Four rooms, one address.

By day she runs talent programmes, builds learner portals, leads product-market strategy, and coordinates government and NGO partnerships for a multi-venture consultancy. By evening she chairs founder meetups, writes essays, and designs event collateral. The scope is not accidental — it is who she is.

01

Programs & Operations

At MHCI, she has built TalentWorks from scratch — legal frameworks, candidate dossiers, placement operations, and a learner portal. She designed TechRise Women Initiative for 250-participant Cohort 1 with the Office of the First Lady (Akwa Ibom State) and AfriLabs. At Zitere Social Enterprises (Triift Africa), she managed the HOME Project in Ogun State. She is Co-Founder of Founder Advocacy Network (FAN).

MHCITalentWorksTechRise WomenTriift AfricaFAN
02

Research & Analysis

Her undergraduate seminar research on gender representation in UNICEF's X posts is in the process of being published in an academic journal — co-authored with her university lecturer. The study found stories centring boys were systematically underrepresented, challenging assumptions about whose vulnerability gets amplified online. Her final-year thesis studied how ThisDay and The Punch covered Nigeria's 2023 fuel subsidy removal. She also writes analytical memos connecting policy and economic data to organisational strategy.

Gender StudiesMedia AnalysisPolicy Research
03

Writing & Voice

Founder of On This Note — essays on culture, faith, womanhood, politics, and the particular texture of being Nigerian and alive. First invited by Jollof & Fiction to speak on Pluralism on their podcast; they later reviewed her essay. Audio companion series VoiceNotes on Spotify. Three years hosting Teens' Time with Chichi on Inspiration FM 105.9. Voice-over work for church sermons and conferences.

SubstackVoiceNotes PodcastRadioVoice-over
04

Events & Ecosystem Building

Patron and lead designer for Founders Friday Uyo — monthly editions since April 2025, including LGA editions with Mark Essien (Eket) and Future Labs (IK). Judge at WTM Uyo IWD 2026. Led Akwa Ibom Tech Week 2025 end-to-end. Has hosted over 30 events across church stages, university festivals, tech conferences, and private occasions. Interested in politics, governance, and the infrastructure of public discourse.

Founders FridayAKTW 2025Church ClothesWTM

She shows up. Every time.

Akwa Ibom Tech Week

MHCI / Ibom Innovation Network

End-to-end delivery lead. Session design, Run of Show, stakeholder management. Hosted the GIFA First Lady’s Breakfast for Women in Tech — the first of its kind in the state. Moderated fireside chat with Defcomm CEO. Hosted Innovation Challenge semi-finals.

2021 — 2024

Teens’ Time with Chichi

9ineteen Network · Inspiration FM 105.9

Three years hosting a weekly youth radio show. Live interviews, curated music, on-air conversations. Launched the show’s Spotify account, managed its Instagram, and coordinated guest appearances including notable public figures.

In Motion

Watch her work.

Founders Friday Uyo

Building a founder ecosystem from the ground up

She designed the framework, built the brand, and has hosted a monthly edition every single month since April 2025. Founders Friday Uyo brings together early-stage founders, builders, and ecosystem enablers in Akwa Ibom — with LGA editions now expanding the reach.

AKTW 2025

Hosting the AKTW Innovation Challenge semi-finals

Young innovators from across Akwa Ibom in front of a live audience and panel — one of the flagship events of Akwa Ibom Tech Week 2025, hosted by Ikanke-Abasi.

AKTW 2025 · GIFA

GIFA First Lady’s Breakfast for Women in Tech

She organised and hosted this breakfast — the first event of its kind in Akwa Ibom State — bringing together women in tech under the patronage of the Office of the First Lady.

TechRise Women Initiative

The First Lady announces TechRise Women

The announcement of TechRise Women Initiative — a programme Ikanke-Abasi designed and manages — by the First Lady of Akwa Ibom State, in partnership with MHCI and AfriLabs.

Writing

On This Note — a publication on living.

Substack · Est. 2024

On This Note

Essays on culture, faith, womanhood, friendship, politics, and the particular texture of being Nigerian and alive. Influenced by Hasan Minhaj’s precision, Zikoko’s warmth, and The Big Daily’s wit.

Read on Substack →

Beyond the Work

She is also this.

Fashion & Design

Clothes are another language

She is deeply interested in fashion as expression and identity. She makes clothes — including what she wore to both editions of Church Clothes, and pieces made for independent creative projects. She has a line of graphic tees in development. Her interest is in how fashion communicates identity, particularly within faith and cultural contexts.

Music & Voice

Choir director. Singer. Worshipper.

Served as choir director for Teens’ Convention at Word of Grace Bible Church, Eket. Taught French at Tonti Model School. Loves gospel music and anything with great storytelling. Music and language feel like the same thing — both are ways of organising feeling into form.

Politics & Discourse

Interested in how power works

Has a genuine interest in politics, governance, and the infrastructure of public discourse — and writes about these things in her essays. Favourite podcast: Hasan Minhaj Doesn’t Know. Enjoys solitaire, tea, and cold pineapple. Has a tendency toward flamboyant word use and considers it a feature.

Currently Reading

Ghostroots

by ‘Pemi Aguda

Twelve stories set in a hauntingly reimagined Lagos — characters vying for freedom from ancestral ties, with supernatural menace woven into the ordinary.

Recommends

Longthroat Memoirs

by Yemisi Aribisala

The book that left an eternal stain — hauntingly beautiful in concept, in stories, in execution. The kind of writing that changes how you write.

Find the book →

Also Recommends

Politics and the English Language

George Orwell · 1946

The essay every writer should read at least once and return to often. On clarity, honesty, and the corrupting relationship between bad prose and bad thinking.

Research

Work that asks harder questions.

In publication process

Gender Representation in UNICEF’s Posts on X: A Content Analysis

Undergraduate seminar research examining how a major international organisation presents childhood through gendered lenses on social media. The study found stories centring boys were systematically underrepresented — challenging assumptions about whose vulnerability gets amplified online. Being published in an academic journal, co-authored with her university lecturer.

Undergraduate Seminar · Communication Arts · University of Uyo
Undergraduate thesis · 2024

Coverage of Nigeria’s Fuel Subsidy Removal by Select Nigerian Newspapers

Content analysis of 79 editions each from ThisDay and The Punch. Found initial high-frequency coverage declining over six months, with framing favouring economic impacts and government perspectives over public voice. Recommended consistent coverage and diverse viewpoints.

B.A. Communication Arts · University of Uyo · 2024 · CGPA 4.14 · Best Graduating Student

University of Uyo · 2024

Best Graduating Student, Communication Arts 2019/2020

Graduated with a 4.14 GPA — the highest in her class. Earlier: President of the Press Club and inaugural President of the ICPC anti-corruption club at secondary school.

Featured in French-language broadcast segment

During her PR internship at Akwa Ibom State Broadcasting Corporation, she was featured on-air in a French-language segment.

Sci-Tech Hackathon South-South · 2023

₦1.5 million 2nd Prize — her first hackathon

Led investor relations for Txone Technologies at the Africa Manufacturing Innovation Hub Sci-Tech Hackathon (South-South). Won ₦1.5 million at the first hackathon she ever entered.
